description_long: This object is an irregularly shaped, reddish-brown stone fragment with a rough, jagged edge and a relatively flat surface on one side. The stone features a prominent circular indentation or ring-like marking on its broadest face, suggesting either natural weathering or possible human modification. The coloration is predominantly a warm terracotta hue, with lighter and darker patches that give it a mottled appearance. The edges are sharp and uneven, indicating it may have broken off from a larger piece. The surface texture appears gritty and coarse, with visible granular details and no signs of polish. This stone would likely be found as a loose obstacle or debris in an urban environment, such as a construction site or along a sidewalk.
description: A reddish-brown, jagged stone fragment with a circular marking and a rough, gritty surface. Its irregular shape and coarse texture make it stand out as a natural or broken urban obstacle.
description_view_0: A thin, edge-on view showing the stone's sharp, jagged profile and minimal width.
description_view_1: The broadest face is visible, featuring a distinct circular indentation and mottled reddish coloration.
description_view_2: Another edge-on view, highlighting the stone's thinness and irregular, uneven edge.
description_view_3: A slightly angled view of the broad face, again showing the circular marking and rough surface texture.
attribute_placement: lying loose on the ground as debris or an obstacle
attribute_pattern: A single, prominent circular marking on one face, with mottled color patches and granular texture throughout.
attribute_weathering: Rough and unpolished, with visible signs of chipping and surface abrasion, indicating exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with smoother, man-made surfaces but may blend with other loose stones or debris in urban environments.
